title: Documentation Copilot Generates Acceptable Suggestions
statement: The learned documentation copilot must successfully generate contextually relevant suggestions for diverse dataset types, achieving >=70% acceptance rate by researchers (P5). Fine-tuned LLM trained on 500+ high-quality datasheet exemplars must exist and function.

title: Generation Assistance
statement: Step 1 of causal mechanism: Generation Assistance: Learned copilot analyzes dataset properties (file formats, distributions, existing metadata) and generates contextual suggestions for missing documentation fields using fine-tuned LLM trained on high-quality datasheet exemplars
falsifier: If suggestion acceptance rate <40%, mechanism fails (assistance not helpful to researchers)
evidence: GitHub Copilot demonstrates AI assistance effectiveness for code; LLMs excel at structured text generation; progressive disclosure reduces decision fatigue

title: Friction Reduction
statement: Step 2 of causal mechanism: Friction Reduction: AI-generated suggestions reduce cognitive load and time required to complete documentation by pre-filling content and providing contextual prompts
falsifier: If time-to-publish doesn't decrease >=30%, friction not meaningfully reduced
evidence: Choice architecture literature shows pre-filled forms have higher completion rates; path of least resistance principle

title: Compliance Preference
statement: Step 3 of causal mechanism: Compliance Preference: When documentation completion becomes easier than circumventing requirements, researchers choose compliance over resistance, leading to higher quality metadata
falsifier: Adversarial testing shows >40% quality degradation when participants are incentivized to minimize effort
evidence: Behavioral economics - people follow default paths when switching costs are high; two-tier validation aligns incentives

title: Language Constraint (English vs. Multilingual)
statement: The copilot system performs effectively for English documentation (>=85% completeness), but performance degrades for multilingual contexts (<70% completeness) due to English-dominant training corpus.
